---
title: "q3"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
#library(skimr)
library(plotly)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(lubridate)
library(zoo)
library(tidyverse)
library(GGally)
library(modelsummary)
library(stargazer)
library(gtsummary)
library(patchwork)
library(glmnet)
library(lmridge)
library(tidyr)
library(caret)
library(MASS)
library(dplyr)
library(interactions)

library(tree)
library(ROCR)


```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
library(ISLR)

data(Auto)
set.seed(1)
train <- sample(1:nrow(Auto), 0.8*nrow(Auto))
train_df=Auto[train,]


row_labels_training = rownames(train_df)
row_labels_all=rownames(Auto)

test_indicies=setdiff(row_labels_all,row_labels_training)
test_data=Auto %>% slice(sapply(test_indicies,as.integer))


all_features=names(Auto)
features=all_features[all_features!='name']

train_df=train_df %>% dplyr::select(any_of(features))
test_df=test_data %>% dplyr::select(any_of(features))
train_df;test_df
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
tree1=tree(mpg~.,data=train_df)
tree1

tree2=tree(mpg~.,data=train_df,mindev=0.001)



set.seed(1)
cv_tree <- cv.tree(tree2)

#plot(cv_tree) # best=10

tree3=prune.tree(tree2, best = 6)
summary(tree2)

cv_tree$size[which.min(cv_tree$dev)]
```

```{r}
plot(cv_tree)
```

```{r}
# RSS 

plot(tree1)
text(tree1, cex = 0.5)

# run predictions

tree1_pred=predict(tree1)
train_yhat=train_df$mpg
train_df$tree1_pred=tree1_pred

low_reg=train_df %>% filter(displacement>190.5& horsepower>127)
low_reg %>% dplyr::select(mpg,tree1_pred)

low_reg_yhat=low_reg %>% dplyr::select(mpg,tree1_pred) %>% pull(tree1_pred)
low_reg_ytrue=low_reg %>% dplyr::select(mpg,tree1_pred) %>% pull(mpg)




mse_func=function(ytrue,y_pred){
  errors=ytrue-y_pred
  mse=mean(errors**2)
  return(mse)
}

ytrue=low_reg_ytrue

sum((ytrue-low_reg_yhat)**2)
```

```{r}
summary(tree2)

plot(tree2)
text(tree2, cex = 0.5)
```

```{r}
summary(tree3)
```

```{r}
predictions_tree1=predict(tree1,newdata=test_df)
predictions_tree2=predict(tree2,newdata=test_df)
predictions_tree3=predict(tree3,newdata=test_df)

ytrue_test=test_df$mpg

mse_func(ytrue_test,predictions_tree1);mse_func(ytrue_test,predictions_tree2);mse_func(ytrue_test,predictions_tree3)
```

```{r}
#Q18

library(MASS)
data(biopsy)
attach(biopsy)
biopsy <- na.omit(biopsy)[-1]


# Y=biopsy %>% mutate(class_num =case_when(class=='benign'~0,
#                                               class=='malignant'~1
#                                               
#   
# )) %>% dplyr::select(class_num) %>% pull()


set.seed(5076)
rand_sel <- sample(nrow(biopsy), 0.7*nrow(biopsy))
train <- biopsy[rand_sel,]
Y_train=train %>% mutate(class_num =case_when(class=='benign'~0,
                                              class=='malignant'~1
                                              
  
)) %>% dplyr::select(class_num) %>% pull()

test <- biopsy[-rand_sel,]

Y_test=test %>% mutate(class_num =case_when(class=='benign'~0,
                                              class=='malignant'~1
                                              
  
)) %>% dplyr::select(class_num) %>% pull()


```

```{r}
log_mod1 <- glm(class ~ ., data = train, family = 'binomial')
```

```{r}
library(ROCR)

phat3 <- predict(log_mod1, test, 'response')


yhat3 <- ifelse(phat3 >= 0.5, 1, 0)
yhat3

pred <- prediction(phat3, Y_test)
perf  <- performance(pred, 'f')

plot(perf, colorize = FALSE, col = 'black')
lines(c(0,1), c(0,1), col = 'gray', lty = 4)

```

```{r}
auc <- performance(pred, measure = 'auc')@y.values[[1]]
round(auc,4)
```

```{r}
test_ts=performance(pred, measure = 'f')@x.values[[1]]
test_fvals=performance(pred, measure = 'f')@y.values[[1]]
best_f1=max(test_fvals[!is.na(test_fvals)])

best_indx=which(test_fvals==best_f1)

best_ts=test_ts[best_indx]

round(best_ts,4);round(best_f1,3)

```

```{r}
#15
library(MASS)
data(biopsy)
attach(biopsy)
biopsy <- na.omit(biopsy)[-1]

set.seed(5076)
rand_sel <- sample(nrow(biopsy), 0.7*nrow(biopsy))
train <- biopsy[rand_sel,]
test <- biopsy[-rand_sel,]

log_mod1 <- glm(class ~ ., data = train, family = 'binomial')

summary(log_mod1)
```

```{r}
train_pred=predict(log_mod1)
train_pred_yhat=ifelse(train_pred>=0.5,1,0)

features=names(test)

test_pred=predict(log_mod1,newdata=test)
test_pred_yhat=ifelse(test_pred>=0.5,1,0)

train$yhat=train_pred_yhat
train$class_num=as.numeric(train$class)-1

train_matches_cnt=train %>% filter(yhat!=class_num) %>% count();train_matches_cnt

err_rate=train_matches_cnt/nrow(train)

round(err_rate,3)

test$yhat=test_pred_yhat
test$class_num=as.numeric(test$class)-1

test_matches_cnt=test %>% filter(yhat!=class_num) %>% count();test_matches_cnt


test_err_rate=test_matches_cnt/nrow(test)

round(test_err_rate,3)


```

```{r}

```
